# Your Success

#### Training Expertise

#### Training Expertise

**Our trainers take your fitness and athleticism goals seriously. We understand that losing weight and gaining muscle is challenging, but that challenge can be enjoyable with the right mindset and support. We analyze your body type and metabolism to create your individualized fitness plan, mainly tailored to your individual biomechanics**.

![](//img1.wsimg.com/isteam/ip/d55347a1-9891-4181-8e76-c6ff0f30dd18/blob-8f8c681.png/:/cr=t:26.91%25,l:0%25,w:100%25,h:46.17%25/rs=w:365,h:365,cg:true)

#### Training Expertise

#### Training Expertise

#### Training Expertise

**With years of success and experience, we are capable of analyzing your strengths and weaknesses and to create a fitness plan that will help you reach your goals. Then, we will work with you to keep you on track and motivated to hit each milestone and target.**

![](//img1.wsimg.com/isteam/ip/d55347a1-9891-4181-8e76-c6ff0f30dd18/blob-6b49ed8.png/:/cr=t:0%25,l:0%25,w:100%25,h:100%25/rs=w:365,h:365,cg:true)

#### Our Goal

#### Training Expertise

#### Our Goal

**We understand that building new fitness habits is difficult and often times we fail. Our goal is to change your view on fitness, so that it becomes a part of who you are and your lifestyle. We strongly believe that if you practice healthy habits, you will be able to obtain a healthy lifestyle.**

Discover a Revolutionary Approach to Healing and Performance with Garrett Salpeter, the founder and CEO of NeuFit®.

# Episode 35: Biomechanics of Elite Athletic Performance with Bert Massey

In this episode, Bert and I speak about the foundation of all athletic performance, efficient movement. We dive into some of the biggest keys of movement, including the ability to load the body unilaterally.    Outside of Olympic weightlifting, powerlifting, and a couple of swimming strokes, almost every movement we care about in sports is asymmetrical or involves unilateral loading (including walking, running, throwing, hitting a ball, etc.). Yet many athletes do not adequately train for strength, speed, endurance, and coordination in these asymmetrical patterns.   Looking through the lens of asymmetrical movement, we discuss the relevant hip, foot, and scapular mechanics and how to train them.

![image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Biomechanics enhances athletes' performance by analyzing movements, optimizing techniques, and tailoring training programs, leading to more efficient energy use and improved results in competitions.
Implementing biomechanical assessments identifies risk factors, enabling customized training and rehabilitation plans that reduce injury risks and enhance athletes' longevity in their sports.

Continuous feedback from athletes and coaches integrates biomechanical insights into training, fostering a collaborative approach to refine techniques and address performance or injury concerns effectively.
